displayTitle: Follow the Grain: Forgings Under the Beam

beats.narration: Forging kneads hot metal into shape — and whatever it cannot heal, it stretches out along the flow. That's your map.
Hot working does three things: it refines the coarse cast grain, heals scattered porosity, and aligns every survivor parallel to grain flow.
Stringers, bursts, and flakes all lie parallel to flow. So aim the beam perpendicular: shafts radially from the outside, disks axially from the face.

payload.heading: Grain flow is the map
payload.note: SHAFT: SCAN RADIAL · DISK: SCAN AXIAL

beats.narration: The signatures: a deep burst steals the back echo, flakes make a forest of small echoes, and surface laps need angle beam or magnetic particle.

chips.t: Lap — angle beam or magnetic particle

beats.narration: So here's the forging rule: flaws follow the flow — send the beam across it, and let the governing code call the verdict.

payload.sub: Perpendicular to grain flow. The code decides.
